title: ODI Matrix — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations

sections.title: Step 1 — Needs gathered (28 entries)
sections.content:
25 market-level needs inherited verbatim from JTBD Phase 3 (`N1`–`N25`). 3 additional product-related needs from FIT Step 10 (PR4, PR5, PR7) injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `product_related=true`. PR1/PR2/PR3/PR6/PR8/PR9 are duplicates of N6/N9/N5/N12/N22/N17 and excluded.

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction recalibration (incumbent-anchored)
sections.content:
Incumbent reference set (from Step 03 + Step 11): **Siemens Smart Infrastructure**, **ABB Ports & Terminals** (Pier 300 LA, ECT Rotterdam 20 MWh), **Wärtsilä GEMS**, **Cavotec** (OPS specialist), **Schneider EcoStruxure**, **Fluence**, OEM-turnkey (Tesla/Sungrow), and non-storage substitute grid-reinforcement.

| # | S_orig | S_recal | Evidence from Step 03 / Step 11 |
|---|---|---|---|
| N8 | 4 | **5** | Siemens/ABB have mature BoP scope control from >100 utility refs — raise S |
| N10 | 2 | **5** | Siemens SI wraps OPS+MV+BESS at public-tender scale — incumbents (not Mein Solar) satisfy this well |
| N11 | 4 | **5** | Cavotec + Siemens jointly own VDE-AR-N 4110 / IEC 80005-1 handover — raise S |
| N13 | 4 | **5** | T1 incumbent witness-test experience on DE grid code — raise S |
| N14 | 5 | **6** | ABB Pier 300 + ECT 20 MWh show >95% OPS connection rate achievable |
| N15 | 4 | **5** | Wärtsilä GEMS / Fluence Mosaic deliver demand-charge shaving — raise S |
| N16 | 5 | **6** | Tier-1 PCS (SMA, Sungrow, ABB) ride-through is mature — raise S |
| N22 | 4 | **5** | CellCube + Tesla recycling partners + EU Battery Passport pilots — raise S |
| N24 | 4 | **5** | T1 incumbents carry cleaner public-tender audit record — raise S |
| PR4 | 4 | **5** | T1 container OEMs (CATL EnerC, Sungrow) offer 1 C profiles at warranty-matched fade — raise S |
| PR5 | 4 | **5** | Siemens/ABB MV protection-relay engineering is mature (IEC 61850 GOOSE) — raise S |
| all others | — | **unchanged** | No Step 03 evidence contradicts original estimate (N1/N4/N7 schedule friction universal; N2/N9/N25 port-specific gaps unfilled by any incumbent) |

Importance scores unchanged (importance-stability rule). 11 of 28 satisfaction scores adjusted; 17 unchanged.

---

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| market_name | Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane Smoothing, Ferry & Terminal-Vehicle Charging |
| naics_code | 488310 |
| total_needs | 28 |
| underserved_count | 14 |
| overserved_count | 0 |
| avg_opportunity_score | 13.14 |

**Top-5 opportunities:**
1. Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS/microgrid capacity by >20% relative to AFIR-2030 peak OPS demand (17.00) — N1
2. Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after tender-award milestone (17.00) — N4
3. Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from tender award to under 15 months to meet AFIR 2030 (17.00) — N7
4. Increase the ability to produce a bankable multi-revenue model (AFIR + §19 StromNEV + HPC + PV firming) (15.00) — N2
5. Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip from Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design for passenger-berth Li-ion containers (15.00) — N9

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-check vs Step 09 Feature-Fit
sections.content:
Feature-Fit avg across 6 tech features (from FIT) = (5.56+5.44+5.44+6.33+7.11+4.89)/6 = **5.80**.
ODI avg = **13.14**.

| Cross-check rule | Triggered? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg >12 AND Feature-Fit avg <4 | **No** (5.80) | FF is mid-range |
| ODI avg <8 AND Feature-Fit avg >7 | No | — |

### Feature-Opportunity Alignment

| Opportunity | Aligned Feature | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **N7 RtB<15 mo (opp 17)** + **N12 firmware (15)** + **N9 VdS fire (15)** | **F5 Grid-Code Cert Delivery (fit 7.11)** | Mein Solar native FGW TR8 path compresses schedule — single strongest tech-feature-opp alignment |
| **N1 sizing (17)** + **N2 multi-rev (15)** + **N15 peak (13)** | **F4 EMS (fit 6.33) + V1 PV+Storage (superior)** | EMS sophistication + PV-EPC heritage address port-wide sizing study needs |
| **N14 OPS>95% (14)** + **N16 PCS ride-through (14)** | **F2 PCS + F3 MV Coupling (both must_be)** | Must_be features already at parity; not differentiators |
| **N10 VOB/VgV (13)** + **N25 AFIR-miss (15)** | **V3 Single-Source EPC (inferior)** | Mein Solar inferior to Siemens at public-port scale — joint-bid required to capture opp |
| **N4 DSO blocker (17)** | No strong feature fit | External complement — MV grid connection capacity; addressable via early DSO engagement |

**Key gap:** Top opportunity N4 (DSO blocker, opp 17) is not directly covered by any Mein Solar feature — it's a complement-level risk (Step 02g). Mitigation = early DSO-engagement workflow on tender entry.

---

sections.content:

| Q1 | `opp = I + max(I−S, 0)` spot-check 5 | PASS (N1 17.00, N4 17.00, N14 14.00, N18 10.00, PR7 11.00) |
| Q2 | Underserved flag iff I>7 AND S<5 | PASS (14 flagged, correct) |
| Q3 | Overserved flag iff I<3 AND S>7 | PASS (none) |
| Q4 | Entry count ≥ Step 06 count (25) | PASS (28 ≥ 25) |
| Q5 | Top-5 sorted desc on opp_score | PASS |
| Q6 | Cross-check vs Step 09 Feature-Fit | PASS (executed; alignment + DSO gap noted) |

---

sections.title: Strategic Takeaways
sections.content:
1. **14/28 needs underserved (50%)** — highest ratio in Mein Solar's portfolio so far; reflects AFIR-2030 urgency and absence of a mature DE public-port BESS EPC category.
2. **Three-way tie at opp=17** (N1, N4, N7) — all schedule-and-sizing risks. Mein Solar wins the schedule axis (F5 Grid-Cert, V3 single-source) but must pair with explicit DSO-early-engagement and parallel-container sizing methodology for 16 MW cruise-berth peaks.
3. **F5 Grid-Code Cert Delivery** is the technology-level winner (fit 7.11) attacking three of the top-7 opportunities (N7, N12, N9).
4. **V1 PV+Storage Design Authority** is the vendor-level winner — activates on N2 (multi-rev), N14 (OPS uptime between vessels), N23 (board-level AFIR story).
5. **V3 Single-Source EPC is inferior** at public-port scale — N10/N25 opportunities captured only via joint-bid with Siemens/ABB/Cavotec for first anchor reference.
6. **N4 DSO blocker** has no in-product mitigation — treat as pre-bid gate; reject tenders without DSO pre-clearance letter to avoid post-award schedule bleeding.

---
